summary: End-to-end backend + frontend testing of OJS MVP. All 34 backend pytest tests passed (100%). Frontend flows verified: home, EN/ID language switching, login for all 4 roles, role-based dashboard sidebar, submit paper form, my papers list, notifications page, public journals archive. Full paper lifecycle (submit -> upload PDF -> assign reviewer -> review -> revision decision -> revision upload -> publish) works end-to-end with proper role-based authorization and confidential-note stripping for authors.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 600+ lines - consider splitting into routers (auth.py, papers.py, reviews.py, users.py, notifications.py) before it grows further.
POST /api/papers allows role='reviewer' to create papers (line 302). If reviewers are not meant to submit, tighten to ('author','admin','editor').
assign-reviewers accepts editor/admin IDs in the valid_ids filter (line 424). If business rule is strictly 'reviewer' role, restrict to role='reviewer' only.
File upload accepts .doc (legacy) while spec says PDF/DOCX only - minor scope clarification.
Storage init key cached in-memory globally; if the integrations storage service rotates, server needs restart. Consider lazy refresh on 401.

CORS allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True is permissive; since cookie auth is enabled, browsers will ignore cookies with wildcard origin. Not breaking since frontend uses Bearer header, but worth tightening to FRONTEND_URL.
send_email on decision/assignment is synchronous within request (fine when mocked). When RESEND_API_KEY is set, large notification fan-outs may slow responses; consider background tasks.
No rate limiting / brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook mentions 5-fail lockout expectation).
test_credentials: admin@ojs.com/admin123, editor@ojs.com/editor123, reviewer@ojs.com/reviewer123, author@ojs.com/author123 - all verified working
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed papers via author@ojs.com. One paper published through full lifecycle and visible on /journals public archive. Temporary registered users are created and deleted within admin tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_ojs_api.py. Run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=... pytest backend/tests/test_ojs_api.py -v. All tests idempotent (no pre-existing state required beyond seeded admin/demo users). The test suite covers public endpoints, auth (login/register/duplicate/role-downgrade/me), users list and admin role-mgmt, full paper lifecycle including storage upload (PDF accepted, non-PDF rejected), reviewer-assignment with role guard, decision flow with author confidential-note masking, revision upload that transitions revision_required -> resubmitted, file download role-based access (author/reviewer/editor/admin), notifications list + mark-all-read, and role-specific stats. Emergent Object Storage integration works - upload_pdf test passes against live storage (EMERGENT_LLM_KEY). Email is MOCKED as designed (RESEND_API_KEY empty -> logs only).
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All spec items verified. Key behaviors confirmed: (1) JWT returned in body AND cookie, Bearer header works. (2) Author registering with role='admin' is silently downgraded to 'author'. (3) Reviewers cannot make decisions (403). (4) Authors cannot see reviews while status in (submitted, under_review); after decision reviews returned with confidential_notes stripped and reviewer_name anonymized to 'Reviewer'. (5) Upload when paper.status=='revision_required' transitions paper to 'resubmitted'. (6) Non-assigned reviewer denied file download (403); admin always allowed. (7) Publish decision makes paper visible on public /papers/published.
